title: referrals — helper functions
description: Ergonomic helpers for accessing the active program from the request context, resolving codes to referral records, and building attribution + reward snapshots. All helpers are thin wrappers around bound services — never business logic.

helpers.purpose: Returns the resolved ReferralProgram for the current request (set by referrals.enforce_active_program middleware). Null when no program context.
helpers.example:
$program = active_referral_program();
if ($program === null) throw new NoActiveProgramException();

helpers.purpose: Returns the resolved ReferralProgram.id or null. Convenience for creators that need the ID without loading the model.
Cache-aware lookup for a code within a tenant. Returns the ReferralCode model (with program loaded) or null when the code is unknown / expired / inactive / usage-cap-reached.
Returns the fraud_score (0..100) from FraudDetector for a Referral row. Read-only wrapper — does NOT create ReferralFraudFlag rows (that's the observer's job).

Given a program's vesting_rule + optional vesting_hold_days / vesting_n_events, computes the vesting_completes_at timestamp for a referral that just reached its trigger event. Handles all five vesting rules.

Constructs the shareable URL for the user's referral code. Uses the tenant's configured `referral_landing_url` + appends ?ref={code}. Falls back to https://{tenant.slug}.academorix.app/?ref={code} when unset.
Returns true when the referrer has NOT hit max_rewards_per_referrer OR max_rewards_per_period for this program. Consulted before creating a Referral row.
Returns true when rewards_issued_count < max_rewards_per_program (when the budget cap is configured). Read from the cached counters (ref:program:budget:{program_id}).
Returns true when the email's domain matches the known-disposable list (data/disposable-email-domains.json). O(1) — the list is loaded into a memoised Set at boot.
Returns the current attribution_snapshot schema version — bumped when the frozen snapshot shape changes. Downstream marketing / finance consumers branch on this.

notes: Helpers deliberately delegate — testing swaps the bound service to a stub, and every helper follows.
No helper directly touches the DB — everything routes through repositories or services.

The FraudDetector-adjacent helper `is_disposable_email` reads from the memoised Set built at boot from the JSON file. New domains added to the list require an app-restart to take effect (rare — the list changes maybe once per quarter).
